compiled_state.title: compute-diff-hash.sh: checkpoint sentinel in staging causes hash churn after record-review.sh
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview
record-review.sh removes .checkpoint-needs-review from staging when a review passes. This changes the staged diff, which changes the diff hash. Any test-gate-status recorded before the review now references a stale hash, causing the pre-commit test gate to block with 'code changed since tests were recorded'.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Have .checkpoint-needs-review in the staged diff (created by a prior interrupted workflow)
2. Run record-test-status.sh — records hash H1 (includes .checkpoint-needs-review)
3. Run the review workflow — record-review.sh removes .checkpoint-needs-review from staging
4. Attempt git commit — test gate blocks: 'Recorded hash H1 does not match current hash H2'
5. Must re-run record-test-status.sh to unblock

## Expected
compute-diff-hash.sh should exclude .checkpoint-needs-review from the hash computation (similar to how .tickets-tracker/ is already excluded), making the hash stable through the review→commit cycle.

## Actual
Hash changes after record-review.sh removes the sentinel, requiring an extra record-test-status.sh pass.

## Fix Candidate
Add .checkpoint-needs-review to the pathspec exclusions in compute-diff-hash.sh.
